name: Google Search Console MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Google Search Console — Google's official tool for monitoring website search performance. Enables AI agents to query search analytics (clicks, impressions, CTR, position), inspect URL indexing status, and analyze search performance data to support SEO analysis and content strategy agents.
use_cases: Querying search performance metrics (clicks, impressions, CTR) from SEO analysis agents
Analyzing keyword ranking data and search query performance from content strategy agents
Checking URL indexing status and coverage errors from web maintenance agents
Monitoring site search performance trends from digital marketing agents
not_for: Sites without Google Search Console configured
Real-time search monitoring (GSC data has 2-3 day lag)
Competitive SEO research on other domains
best_when: A website owner, SEO specialist, or content marketer wants AI agents to analyze Google Search Console data — understanding what queries drive traffic, which pages rank well, and identifying SEO opportunities.
avoid_when: Your site is new or has no GSC data yet. Also note GSC data is delayed 2-3 days — not suitable for real-time monitoring.

auth.notes: Google OAuth 2.0 with Search Console read scopes. Requires Google account with Search Console access. OAuth credentials via Google Cloud Console.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Google Search Console API is free; quota limits apply
pricing.notes: Google Search Console is free. Community MCP by ahonn. GSC API quota: 2,000 requests/day default.

known_agent_gotchas: GSC data has 2-3 day lag — not suitable for real-time queries
OAuth token refresh required — implement OAuth refresh flow for long-running agents
Search Console API filters are complex — agents need to understand dimension/metric model
Data is limited to 16 months history in GSC — plan agent queries accordingly
agent_readiness.security_notes: Google OAuth 2.0. HTTPS to Google APIs. Search Console data includes website traffic patterns — sensitive competitive information. Use minimal OAuth scopes (read-only).

security_score_components.security_notes: Google OAuth with read-only GSC scopes. Website traffic data is business-sensitive. Google's security infrastructure applies.
